description: E2E happy path (integration): discovery → /authorize with an existing Alga session + PKCE → consent → /token → call POST /api/mcp with the Alga-issued bearer and successfully invoke a tool scoped to the user's tenant/permissions.
PKCE enforced: /authorize rejects requests with no code_challenge or method=plain; /token rejects a wrong/missing code_verifier (invalid_grant).
redirect_uri must match exactly: a mismatch is rejected at both /authorize and /token.
Authorization code is single-use and expiring: replay and expired-code exchanges are rejected.
Refresh rotation: refresh_token grant issues a new access token and a new refresh token; the old refresh token is then invalid (replay detected).
Audience binding: the RS rejects an Alga-signed token whose aud is not the MCP resource; accepts one bound to <base>/api/mcp.
Token represents the user (integration): a dispatched tool call the user IS permitted succeeds; one the user is NOT permitted is denied by RBAC.
Revocation (DB-backed integration): after /oauth/revoke (or disconnect), both the access token and refresh token stop working and the grant is marked revoked in the DB.
Backward compat: a legacy external-IdP agent token still authenticates and dispatches via the existing path (regression guard).
Discovery documents are standards-valid and mutually consistent: AS metadata advertises S256, the endpoints + jwks resolve, and PRM points at Alga-as-AS with matching scopes.
CIMD: a URL client_id resolves a metadata document and the requested redirect_uri is validated against it; mismatch is rejected; the client record is persisted.
CIMD SSRF guard: non-https or disallowed-host client_id URLs are rejected without fetching internal resources.
Connected-clients UI (integration): a user sees their authorized clients and a disconnect action revokes the grant (reflected in the DB and subsequent calls).
EE gate: all new AS/revoke endpoints return 404/disabled in CE and resolve via the seam in EE.
Unauthenticated /authorize redirects to Alga login and returns to complete the authorization round-trip afterward.
Consent: first connect shows the consent screen; per policy, a subsequent connect by the same (user, client) is remembered (trust-on-first-use).
DB sanity (integration, happy + guard): client and grant records persist against migrated schema with tenant scoping; a malformed/duplicate insert is rejected by constraints.
